revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Used substitution to import all templates here relatively easily. On English Wikipedia, if you want the templates, extract them from this page to create templates. The Japanese Wikipedia system is too difficult. {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=Page target for move is at List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=Page target for move is at {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province|de|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province|ja|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province|zh|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province}} {{afc comment|1=Page target for move is at List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=Switching to deprecate the templates. Therefore, things might be slightly unexpected. {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X", then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise, I have not. Translation of the texts will follow later, as it is much less difficult than the tables. Exception applies if another comment explains the exception below. {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 05:35, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 09:49, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}} – {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 09:48, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 09:48, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 05:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''List of Shikinai Shrines in Mimasaka Province''' (みまさかのくにのしきないしゃいちらん) is a list of shrines recorded in the 9th and 10th volumes, "Upper and Lower Jinmyōchō", of the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳}}, that is, the shrines known as {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|de|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|zh|式内社}} and their associated {{ill|Ronsya|de|Ronsya|ja|論社|zh|論社}}, which are classified under {{ill|Mimasaka Province|de|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|zh|美作国}}. It also includes those shrines that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|zh|延喜式}} Jinmyōchō but were not recorded in it, the so‐called {{ill|Shikigai Shrine|de|Shikigai Shrine|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|zh|式外社}}. == Shikinai Shrines == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''{{ill|Oba District|de|Oba District|ja|大庭郡|zh|大庭郡}}''' 8 groups (regular minor) |- |'''Sahara Shrine''' || saharano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Katahe Shrine''' || kataheno || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Ichia Shrine Second Group''' || ichiahano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Yokomino Shrine''' || yokomino || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Kutono Shrine''' || kutono || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Usakamino Shrine''' || usakamino
unakami || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Nakata Shrine''' || nakatano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''{{ill|Tomatō District|de|Tomatō District|ja|苫東郡|zh|苫東郡}}''' 2 groups (1 major group, 1 minor group) |- |rowspan=2|'''Takano Shrine''' || rowspan=2| kakuyano
takano || rowspan=2| Shikinai Shosha || rowspan=2| || rowspan=2| ({{ill|candidate shrines|de|candidate shrines|ja|論社|zh|candidate shrines|lt=candidate|12=draft}}) {{ill|Takano Shrine|de|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|zh|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|lt=Takano Shrine|lt_ja=高野神社|lt_de=Takano Shrine|lt_zh=高野神社}} || {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|de|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|zh|岡山県}} {{ill|Tsuyama City|de|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|zh|津山市}} Ninomiya || Mimasaka Province Ninomiya || rowspan=2| |- || ({{ill|candidate shrines|de|candidate shrines|ja|論社|zh|candidate shrines|lt=candidate|12=draft}}) {{ill|Takano Shrine|de|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|zh|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|lt=Takano Shrine|lt_ja=高野神社|lt_de=Takano Shrine|lt_zh=高野神社}} || {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|de|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|zh|岡山県}} {{ill|Tsuyama City|de|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|zh|津山市}} Takano Hongo |- |'''Nakayama Shrine''' || chiusanno

It also includes those shrines that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|zh|延喜式}} Jinmyōchō but were not recorded in it, the so‐called {{ill|Shikigai Shrine|de|Shikigai Shrine|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|zh|式外社}}. == Shikinai Shrines == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''{{ill|Oba District|de|Oba District|ja|大庭郡|zh|大庭郡}}''' 8 groups (regular minor) |- |'''Sahara Shrine''' || saharano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Katahe Shrine''' || kataheno || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Ichia Shrine Second Group''' || ichiahano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Yokomino Shrine''' || yokomino || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Kutono Shrine''' || kutono || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Usakamino Shrine''' || usakamino

{{afc comment|1=Used substitution to import all templates here relatively easily. On English Wikipedia, if you want the templates, extract them from this page to create templates. The Japanese Wikipedia system is too difficult. {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=Page target for move is at List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=Page target for move is at {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province|de|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province|ja|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province|zh|List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province}} {{afc comment|1=Page target for move is at List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=Switching to deprecate the templates. Therefore, things might be slightly unexpected. {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X", then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise, I have not. Translation of the texts will follow later, as it is much less difficult than the tables. Exception applies if another comment explains the exception below. {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 05:35, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 09:49, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}} – {{ill|Immanuelle|de|Immanuelle|ja|Immanuelle|zh|Immanuelle}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 09:48, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 09:48, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 05:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC)}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|de|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|ユーザー・トーク:Immanuelle|zh|User talk:Immanuelle}}) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''List of Shikinai Shrines in Mimasaka Province''' (みまさかのくにのしきないしゃいちらん) is a list of shrines recorded in the 9th and 10th volumes, "Upper and Lower Jinmyōchō", of the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}}, that is, the shrines known as {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|de|Shikinai Shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|zh|式内社}} and their associated {{ill|Ronsya|de|Ronsya|ja|論社|zh|論社}}, which are classified under {{ill|Mimasaka Province|de|Mimasaka Province|ja|美作国|zh|美作国}}. It also includes those shrines that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|zh|延喜式}} Jinmyōchō but were not recorded in it, the so‐called {{ill|Shikigai Shrine|de|Shikigai Shrine|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|zh|式外社}}. == Shikinai Shrines == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! revision.text: This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in Mimasaka Province which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of LALALAList of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. Or the list of shrines in Mimasaka Province which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location bottom]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in Mimasaka Province which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of Mimasaka Province which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! Or the list of shrines in Mimasaka Province which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of Mimasaka Province which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! Or the list of shrines in Mimasaka Province which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of Mimasaka Province which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! [[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! revision.text:
[[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion.[[Category:edited lists]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! revision.text:
[[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion.[[Category:edited lists]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! revision.text:
[[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion.[[Category:edited lists]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''{{ill|Oba District|de|Oba District|ja|大庭郡|zh|大庭郡}}''' 8 groups (regular minor) |- |'''Sahara Shrine''' || saharano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Katahe Shrine''' || kataheno || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Ichia Shrine Second Group''' || ichiahano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Yokomino Shrine''' || yokomino || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Kutono Shrine''' || kutono || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Usakamino Shrine''' || usakamino
unakami || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |'''Nakata Shrine''' || nakatano || Shikinai Shosha || || || || || || || |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''{{ill|Tomatō District|de|Tomatō District|ja|苫東郡|zh|苫東郡}}''' 2 groups (1 major group, 1 minor group) |- |rowspan=2|'''Takano Shrine''' || rowspan=2| kakuyano
takano || rowspan=2| Shikinai Shosha || rowspan=2| || rowspan=2| ({{ill|candidate shrines|de|candidate shrines|ja|論社|zh|candidate shrines|lt=candidate}}) {{ill|Takano Shrine|de|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|zh|高野神社 (津山市二宮)|lt=Takano Shrine|lt_ja=高野神社|lt_de=Takano Shrine|lt_zh=高野神社}} || {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|de|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|zh|岡山県}} {{ill|Tsuyama City|de|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|zh|津山市}} Ninomiya || Mimasaka Province Ninomiya || rowspan=2| |- || ({{ill|candidate shrines|de|candidate shrines|ja|論社|zh|candidate shrines|lt=candidate}}) {{ill|Takano Shrine|de|Takano Shrine|ja|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|zh|高野神社 (津山市高野本郷)|lt=Takano Shrine|lt_ja=高野神社|lt_de=Takano Shrine|lt_zh=高野神社}} || {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|de|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|zh|岡山県}} {{ill|Tsuyama City|de|Tsuyama City|ja|津山市|zh|津山市}} Takano Hongo |- |'''Nakayama Shrine''' || chiusanno

amano- || Shikinai Shosha || || Ame Ishimonbetsu Shrine || {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|de|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|zh|岡山県}} {{ill|Mimasaka City|de|Mimasaka City|ja|美作市|zh|美作市}} Takinomiya || Mimasaka Province Sannomiya || |} == Shikigai Shrines == {{Section stub}} Shrines that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|zh|延喜式}} Jinmyōchō but were not recorded '''美作国の式内社一覧'''（みまさかのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[延喜式]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[美作国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。 また『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが同帳に記載の無い神社、いわゆる「[[式外社]]」についても付記する。 == 式内社 == {{節stub}} 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社1座1社（名神大社）・小社10座9社の計11座10社を記載。 {{式内社一覧/凡例}} {{式内社一覧/header}} |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[大庭郡]]''' 8座（並小） |- |'''佐波良神社'''||サハラノ||小|||||||||| |- |'''形部神社'''||カタヘノ||小|||||||||| |- |'''壱粟神社 二座'''||イチアハノ||小|||||||||| |- |'''横見神社'''||ヨコミノ||小|||||||||| |- |'''久刀神社'''||クトノ||小|||||||||| |- |'''兎上神社'''||ウサカミノ

[[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion.[[Category:edited lists]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} ! revision.text:
[[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[Category:Shikinaisha top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion.[[Category:edited lists]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! [[Category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]][[Category: Lists of Shikinaisha by location top]]This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} ! This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} ! This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} ! This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinai Shrines (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! revision.comment: Text replacement - "Shikinai Shrines" to "Shikinaisha"
revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the {{ill|Engishiki|de|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|zh|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation | | |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! revision.text:
This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608745]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} This is a '''List of Shikinaisha in Mimasaka Province'''. Or the list of shrines in [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]] which is present in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. On this page we will also cover the {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka Province]] which are shrines that are known to have existed at that time due to other historical records, such as the [[Rikkokushi]], but for some reason were excluded from the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. We will cover them in a separate list to avoid confusion. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {{Section stub}} In the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] Jinmyōchō, a total of 11 groups and 10 shrines are recorded – one major shrine group (Myojin Taisha) and ten minor shrine groups (comprising nine shrines). {{List of Shikinaisha/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation | | |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! [[:ja:美作国の式内社一覧]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:55, 21 November 2025 (UTC)}}
This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Mimasaka Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Mimasaka Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Mimasaka Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Mimasaka Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Mimasaka Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Mimasaka Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shōsha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 11 Shikinaisha in the province – 10 Shikinai Shosha and 1 Shikinai Taisha.
